claims.assertion: Action-space entropy below 3 bits/dim indicates high trajectory redundancy and is a risk signal for weak policy generalisation to out-of-distribution states
claims.conditions: entropy computed as marginal Shannon entropy over discretised action dimensions; threshold applies per-dimension
claims.caution: Treat as a useful heuristic, not a validated law. Some tasks legitimately require low entropy (e.g. peg insertion). Some tasks fail despite high entropy. Control mode distorts interpretation. The link to downstream OOD failure has NOT been directly measured — it is inferred from first principles. Requires ablation studies on held-out test sets before upgrading to active_hypothesis.

evidence.notes: 2D velocity-command, entropy 5.30 bits/dim — healthy coverage across push target space
14-DOF position-command, entropy 4.45 bits/dim — moderate coverage for a constrained peg-insertion task
14-DOF position-command real hardware, entropy 4.24 bits/dim — narrower distribution than sim but above warning threshold

DROID, 7-DOF real hardware, 15Hz, diverse tasks and robots, 100 episodes. Action entropy 4.24 bits/dim — above the 3-bit warning threshold, indicating healthy action-space coverage across diverse environments. Fourth evidence point; all four datasets sit in the 4.2–5.3 range, with no dataset near the 3-bit risk threshold.

BridgeData V2, 7-DOF real hardware velocity-command, 5Hz, 50415 episodes, 22k distinct tasks. Action entropy 1.98 bits/dim — below the 3-bit warning threshold despite enormous task diversity. Likely cause: at 5Hz the action values cluster in a narrow velocity range (slow motions dominate), collapsing the distribution. First real-world data point BELOW the 3-bit threshold, directly supporting ENT-001's assertion that sub-3-bit entropy is a detectable risk signal. Upgrades claim evidence count to 5.
Real hardware mobile ALOHA, 14-DOF, 50Hz, shrimp cooking, 18 episodes. Entropy 4.32 bits/dim — above 3-bit warning threshold. Mobile platform with task variety produces healthy coverage.
Human teleop sim, 14-DOF, 50Hz, cube transfer, 50 episodes. Entropy 4.68 bits/dim — above warning threshold.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF, 50Hz, battery task. Entropy 4.69 bits/dim — healthy.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF, 50Hz, candy task. Entropy 4.28 bits/dim — above threshold, lowest of the static ALOHA tasks.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF, 50Hz, coffee task. Entropy 4.88 bits/dim — highest of static ALOHA tasks; coffee prep involves diverse pouring angles and speeds.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF, 50Hz, cups task. Entropy 4.68 bits/dim — healthy.
SO-100 low-cost arm, real hardware, pick and place, 50 episodes. Entropy 4.52 bits/dim — above threshold.
SO-100 low-cost arm, real hardware, block stacking, 56 episodes. Entropy 4.79 bits/dim — healthy.

interpretation_rule.ok_text: Healthy action-space coverage. Diverse trajectory distribution — policy likely to generalise.
interpretation_rule.warning_text: Moderate coverage. Some risk of limited trajectory diversity. Check whether demonstrations span the full operating range of the task.
interpretation_rule.critical_text: Low entropy — possible mode collapse or narrow trajectory variation. Policy expected to generalise poorly to out-of-distribution object placements.
interpretation_rule.note: MEDIUM confidence: metric is validated on 3 sim/real datasets but the link to downstream generalisation failure has not been directly measured on held-out test sets. Treat as a risk signal, not a guarantee.

falsification.condition: A dataset with action entropy below 3 bits/dim where the trained policy achieves strong OOD generalisation, OR a dataset with entropy above 4.5 bits/dim where the policy fails OOD

pending_tests.reason: Multi-task sim dataset — would characterise entropy range for constrained skill-specific demonstrations

claims.assertion: PCA top-2 variance fraction above 0.95 in high-dimensional action spaces (> 6 DOF) signals effective dimensionality collapse — the policy will likely only need 1–2 latent modes
claims.conditions: PCA computed over all steps concatenated across episodes; top-2 eigenvectors capture fraction of variance

evidence.notes: 14-DOF bimanual sim, 50Hz, peg insertion. PCA top-2 variance fraction 0.669 — well below the 0.95 threshold. Demonstrates that a precise high-DOF constrained task does NOT show dimensionality collapse despite leveraging structured bimanual coordination. First evidence point for ENT-002.

DROID, 7-DOF real hardware, 15Hz, diverse tasks. PCA top-2 fraction 0.566 — lowest of all profiled datasets, reflecting high effective dimensionality from cross-task and cross-robot diversity. Strongly below 0.95 threshold. Second evidence point; confirms the threshold does not fire for healthy diverse high-DOF datasets.

BridgeData V2, 7-DOF real hardware velocity-command, 5Hz, 50415 episodes. PCA top-2 variance fraction 0.989 — above the 0.95 threshold in a 7-DOF action space. This is the FIRST dataset to fire the ENT-002 warning. Despite 22k distinct tasks and diverse robot configurations, the velocity commands at 5Hz collapse almost entirely onto 2 principal components. This confirms the claim: a high PCA fraction in a high-DOF space is detectable and meaningful as a redundancy indicator. Upgrades confidence from LOW-MODERATE to MODERATE.
Real hardware mobile ALOHA, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.686 — well below 0.95. Mobile ALOHA with diverse cooking motions uses many action dimensions effectively.
Human teleop sim, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.737 — well below 0.95.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.783 — below 0.95.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.529 — lowest of all new datasets; candy manipulation uses a broad range of joint configurations.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.663 — well below 0.95.
Static ALOHA real hardware, 14-DOF. PCA top-2 fraction 0.872 — below 0.95 threshold but elevated; cups task involves more repetitive grasping motions.

SO-100 low-cost arm, real hardware, pick and place, 50 episodes. PCA top-2 fraction 0.913 — below 0.95 but the closest approach yet among healthy datasets. Low-DOF arm (6-DOF) and stereotyped pick-place motion naturally concentrates variance. Does not trigger the warning threshold.

SO-100 low-cost arm, real hardware, block stacking, 56 episodes. PCA top-2 fraction 0.933 — second closest approach to 0.95 threshold. Together with pickplace (0.913), SO-100 datasets consistently cluster near the boundary; the 0.95 threshold appears well-placed to separate healthy low-DOF arms from true dimensionality collapse (BridgeData2 at 0.989).

falsification.condition: A task where PCA top-2 variance > 0.95 but the policy requires all DOF for task completion
